name: sf-insights
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /ren:insights. Validates the read-only invariant (no files written), the four-section synthesis output, empty-window tolerance, and --days respect.

tests.expected_output_summary: Collector mines the last 30 days of local sessions; the synthesis renders a four-section report grounded in the collected facts.
binary_assertions: The collector is invoked read-only (scripts/collect.py) with the default 30-day window
User-facing output contains all four section headers: '## Usage at a Glance', '## What's Working', '## What's Hindering', '## Quick Wins'
Every quantitative claim in the report traces to a number/tool/project present in the collector's '=== SF-INSIGHTS COLLECTED DATA (v1) ===' block
No file anywhere on disk is created, modified, or deleted by the run (read-only invariant)

tests.expected_output_summary: Look-back window narrows to 7 days; only source files with mtime within 7 days are scanned.
binary_assertions: The collector is invoked with --days 7 (window_days: 7 appears in the collected block)
Source files older than 7 days (by mtime) are excluded from the report
User-facing output still contains the four section headers
No file is written, modified, or deleted during the run

tests.expected_output_summary: Only sessions whose project (cwd basename) matches 'sidecar' are included.
binary_assertions: The collected block reports project_filter: sidecar
Every session listed under '## SESSIONS' has project matching 'sidecar' (case-insensitive substring)
No file is written, modified, or deleted during the run

tests.expected_output_summary: When no local sessions fall within the window, the skill renders all four sections noting the empty window rather than inventing activity or erroring.
binary_assertions: When the collected block shows 'sessions_found: 0', the report still contains all four section headers
The report states plainly that the window is empty and suggests widening --days (no fabricated sessions, dates, or trends)
The run exits successfully (no crash, no stack trace) on an empty window
No file is written, modified, or deleted during the run

tests.expected_output_summary: A transcript containing malformed JSON lines is parsed tolerantly: bad lines are skipped, valid records still summarized, no crash.
binary_assertions: Malformed JSONL lines are skipped without aborting the file or the run
Valid records from the same file still appear in the collected block (tool/topic/error signal)
No error or stack trace appears in the user-facing output

non_triggers.prompt: /ren:recall what did we decide about auth
